scenario: Catalyst Logistics starts 14000 transactions per day in Camunda 8.8 from a Java service. The team uses the official Java client and must start a process by its bpmnProcessId, send a variables map, and use the latest deployed version of the definition without explicitly looking up version numbers.
question: Which Java client fluent call starts a process instance with the latest version in Camunda 8?

options.text: client.newCreateInstanceCommand().bpmnProcessId("order").latestVersion().variables(map).send() resolves the latest version on the broker and returns a future
client.newCreateInstanceCommand().processId("order").variables(map).send() resolves the latest version by default and is the documented fluent path
client.deploy("order").startInstance(map).send() deploys-and-starts in one call and is the documented Java client fluent path in 8.8
client.startProcess("order", map) is a static helper that resolves the latest version internally and is the documented Java client surface

a.text: Correct. The Camunda 8 Java client uses a fluent builder: newCreateInstanceCommand().bpmnProcessId(...).latestVersion().variables(...).send().

b.text: Incorrect. The fluent step is bpmnProcessId not processId, and latestVersion() must be called explicitly.

c.text: Incorrect. There is no deploy-and-start fluent call in the Java client; deploy and start are separate commands.

d.text: Incorrect. The Java client uses a fluent builder; there is no static client.startProcess helper of that signature.

explanation: The Java client starts an instance via newCreateInstanceCommand().bpmnProcessId(...).latestVersion().variables(...).send().
